questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Earthquakes occur when tectonic plates shift suddenly. Q: The point of origin is called the epicentre. R: Seismic waves travel through the Earth's crust. S: The Richter scale measures the magnitude of earthquakes.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: It is high time we leave for the station.
options: It is high time we leave for the station.
It is high time we have left for the station.
It is high time we were leaving for the station.
It is high time we left for the station.
questions.solution: It is high time we left for the station.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ the circumstances, we decided to postpone.

questions.stem: Correct: Each of the participants were given a certificate.
options: Every of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants was given a certificate.
Each of the participant was given a certificate.
questions.solution: Each of the participants was given a certificate.

The police arrested the suspect last night.
The police has arrested the suspect last night.
The police had arrested the suspect last night.
questions.solution: The police arrested the suspect last night.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The effectiveness-NTU method:
options: Used when inlet/outlet temperatures are not fully known
questions.solution: ε = Q_actual/Q_max. NTU = UA/C_min. ε = f(NTU, C_r, flow arrangement).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Varignon's theorem states:
options: Moment of resultant = sum of moments of components
questions.solution: Varignon: moment of resultant = Σ moments of forces.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slow, continuous deformation of a material under constant load at high temperature is called:
questions.solution: Creep is time-dependent plastic strain that becomes significant at high temperature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A shaft transmits a torque of 150 N·m at 1200 rpm. The power transmitted is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Moment of inertia depends on:
options: Mass distribution about the axis of rotation
questions.solution: I = ∫r²dm. Parallel axis theorem: I = I_G + md². Perpendicular axis: I_z = I_x + I_y.

questions.stem: NTU in heat exchanger analysis is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Orifice meter:
options: Sharp-edged opening. Q = C_d·A₀√(2ΔP/ρ). C_d ~ 0.6. Higher loss than venturi
questions.solution: Orifice: vena contracta. C_c = contraction coefficient. C_v = velocity coefficient. C_d = C_c·C_v.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Preloaded bolted joint: external load causes bolt to carry:
options: Portion proportional to joint stiffness ratio

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Knocking in SI engine occurs when:
options: End gas auto-ignites before flame front reaches
questions.solution: Knock: rapid pressure rise from auto-ignition.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Carnot engine takes 1000 kJ from a source at 1000 K and rejects to 400 K. The work output is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Casting — gating system:
options: Pouring basin → sprue → runner → ingate → cavity. Pressurized vs non-pressurized
questions.solution: Pressurized: gate < sprue bottom (turbulent). Non-pressurized: gate > sprue bottom (less turb.).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ferrous materials contain:
questions.solution: Ferrous: steel, cast iron. Non-ferrous: Al, Cu, Mg, Ti alloys.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A shaft transmits a torque of 200 N·m at 600 rpm. The power transmitted is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Free body diagram:
options: A diagram showing all external forces acting on a body
A diagram of all internal forces
questions.solution: FBD: isolate body, show all forces/moments. Essential for equilibrium analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a point load of 40 kN at mid-span. The max bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Reynolds number (Re) is:
questions.solution: Re = ρVL/μ = VL/ν. Laminar Re < 2300 (pipe), turbulent Re > 4000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Freon R-134a is preferred over R-12 because:
questions.solution: R-134a: zero ODP vs R-12 which depletes ozone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pressure at depth 8 m in water (ρ = 1000 kg/m³) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Brakes — band brake:
options: T = (T₁-T₂)·r. T₁/T₂ = e^(μθ). θ = wrap angle (radians)
questions.solution: Self-energizing: T₁/T₂ increases. Self-locking: when e^(μθ) > certain limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Brake thermal efficiency:
options: Brake power / (fuel mass flow × calorific value)
questions.solution: η_bth = BP/(ṁ_f × CV). η_ith = IP/(ṁ_f × CV). Mechanical η = BP/IP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Carnot engine takes 1500 kJ from a source at 900 K and rejects to 400 K. The work output is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shear angle (Merchant) — minimum energy:
options: φ = 45° + α/2 - β/2. β = arctan(μ). α = rake angle
questions.solution: Higher φ = thinner chip = less energy. Lower φ = thicker chip = more energy. Sticking + sliding friction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbon steel with 0.4% C is:
options: Medium carbon steel (used for shafts, gears, rails)
questions.solution: Low C (<0.3%): ductile, sheets. Medium (0.3-0.6%): heat-treatable, machine parts. High (>0.6%): tools.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A shaft transmits a torque of 100 N·m at 1500 rpm. The power transmitted is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A couple consists of:
options: Two equal forces in same direction
questions.solution: Couple: two equal, opposite, parallel forces (pure moment).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Second law — Clausius:
options: Heat flows from cold to hot
Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot. δQ/T = dS
questions.solution: Clausius: requires work input for refrigeration. Q_cold + W = Q_hot.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simple truss with apex load 10 kN and span 3m. The force (kN) in one principal rafter (θ=30°) is:
questions.solution: Member force = 10.0 kN (by method of joints)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Entropy is a measure of:
questions.solution: S = k·ln(Ω). Higher entropy = more disorder. ΔS = ∫dQ_rev/T.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Newton's second law:
questions.solution: F = ma = m dv/dt = d(mv)/dt. Impulse = FΔt = Δ(mv).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Welding — SAW:
options: Submerged arc. Flux blanket. High deposition, no spatter. Flat/horizontal only
questions.solution: SAW: automatic. Uses granular flux. High quality on thick plates (shipbuilding, pipes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 3 m carries a UDL of 10 kN/m. The max bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A psychrometric chart plots:
questions.solution: Psychrometric chart: ω (specific humidity) vs DBT.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rolling contact bearings:
questions.solution: Bearing life: L₁₀ = (C/P)^p × 10⁶ rev. p=3 (ball), p=10/3 (roller). L₁₀ life: 90% reliability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a steam power plant, the condenser maintains:
questions.solution: Condenser: low back pressure for maximum work.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sunk key is said to be fitting if its:
options: Top and bottom faces are parallel
questions.solution: Saddle key: top & bottom tapered; sunk key: fits in keyway.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An Otto cycle has compression ratio 10, γ = 1.35. Efficiency (%) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soderberg line:
options: σ_a/Se + σ_m/σ_y = 1. Safe for infinite life
questions.solution: Soderberg: conservative. Goodman: moderate. Gerber: best fit. Modified Goodman: includes yield.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Klein's construction is used to find:
questions.solution: Klein's construction → acceleration of slider in slider-crank.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Balancing of rotating masses:
options: Reduces vibrations by canceling centrifugal forces
questions.solution: Static balance: sum of centrifugal forces = 0. Dynamic balance: also sum of moments = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The main function of a flywheel is to:
options: Control mean speed for load change
Reduce fluctuation of speed over a cycle
questions.solution: A flywheel stores/returns energy to smooth speed fluctuations; a governor controls mean speed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Enthalpy of moist air:
questions.solution: h: sensible (1.005T) + latent (ω·2501) + superheat (ω·1.86T). Psychrometric chart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rankine cycle:
options: Steam power plant. Pump → boiler → turbine → condenser. η = (W_turbine - W_pump)/Q_boiler
questions.solution: Rankine: 4 components. Reheat + regeneration improve efficiency. Supercritical: higher η.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The polytropic index n for an isothermal process is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Triple point of water:
questions.solution: Triple point: unique T,P where solid, liquid, vapor coexist in equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a constant-volume (isochoric) process the work done by a closed system is:
questions.solution: W = ∫p dV = 0 when volume is constant, so all heat changes internal energy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The zeroth law of thermodynamics defines the concept of:
questions.solution: If two bodies are each in thermal equilibrium with a third, they are with each other — the basis of temperature.

questions.solution: Continuity speeds up the flow; Bernoulli then drops the pressure as velocity rises.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For most engineering metals, the theoretical upper limit of Poisson's ratio is:
questions.solution: ν = 0.5 corresponds to an incompressible material; real metals are ≈ 0.25–0.35.

questions.stem: Head loss due to friction in a pipe of length 10 m, dia 50 mm, f = 0.02, v = 5 m/s:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the depth of a rectangular beam is doubled (same width and load), its bending strength (resisting moment) increases by a factor of:
questions.solution: Section modulus Z = bd²/6 ∝ d², so the moment capacity rises 2² = 4×.

questions.stem: Pump specific speed:
options: N_s = N√Q / H^0.75. Identifies pump type (centrifugal, mixed, axial)
questions.solution: N_s: radial (500-4000), mixed (4000-10000), axial (>10000). Affinity laws.

questions.stem: Principal stresses:
options: Maximum, intermediate, minimum normal stresses (σ₁ > σ₂ > σ₃). No shear on principal planes
questions.solution: Principal planes: τ = 0. 3D stress state. Mohr's circle: graphical representation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the simply supported beam shown (central load W = 30 kN, span L = 6 m), the maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: For a central point load, M_max = WL/4 = 30×6/4 = 45 kN·m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Helical spring deflection:
options: δ = 8FD³N / (Gd⁴). N = number of active coils
questions.solution: Stiffness k = F/δ = Gd⁴/(8D³N). Series: 1/k_eq = Σ1/k_i. Parallel: k_eq = Σk_i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Center of pressure for a vertical rectangular surface is:
questions.solution: Center of pressure is always below the centroid for a vertical surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Relative humidity is:
options: The ratio of water to air
The ratio of actual vapor pressure to saturation vapor pressure at same temperature
questions.solution: RH = P_v/P_sat × 100%. RH 100% = saturated air (fog, rain).
